name: Jessica Morse
subject: Still Serving
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jessica Morse for Congress, a grassroots campaign
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/MGNShtOoSJq-MbygT0SYy4zVf7zgc0D1zRXxLcD6GcM=/SzNRFlKNqt839qE9PEmseG0-T-PhxbAH0BXelz1-c7A=?recurring=auto&refcode=email_ngp_fr_20181105_JM_20181105-jmfinal_all_logo)
Dear Peter,
I am proud to announce that I was recently appointed by Governor Newsom to serve as Deputy Secretary for Forest Resources Management at California's Natural Resources Agency. I am working with incredible public servants and I'm part of a collaborative community, focused on arresting the cycle of catastrophic wildfires ravaging California.

Looking back on the 2018 election, I realized that although we didn't win a seat in Congress, we succeeded in our deeper mission -- serving California.

Together, we elevated the idea that the long-term solution to catastrophic wildfire included opportunities to create rural jobs, expand economic innovation, restore watersheds, and promote carbon sequestration. The fact that a good environment can promote a good economy was rarely mentioned in Sierra communities. Now it's the opening line. I am honored to have the opportunity to help make this vision a reality.

Thinking back on the underlying goals that propelled me to run for Congress, I realized that we accomplished them:
\- I wanted to flip the House so Congress could serve as a constitutional check on the executive branch;
\- I wanted to elect more women; and
\- While there were plenty of issues that I was eager to champion in Congress, I wanted to lead efforts on forest health
and wildfire solutions.

My current role may actually have a bigger impact on mitigating catastrophic wildfires than I would have had in Congress.

I wouldn't have had this opportunity to serve without your support. Your dedication, generosity, and energy throughout the campaign set a strong foundation of clear thinking and public service in our community that we will continue build upon for decades to come.

It was an honor to campaign with you and it is an honor to continue to serve you in my new role.

Thank you again,

Jessica
** **
Paid for by Jessica Morse for Congress, a grassroots campaign.
---
Jessica Morse for Congress
1079 Sunrise Ave
Ste B 275
Roseville CA 95661 United States
_If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please[unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/MGNShtOoSJq-MbygT0SYy4zVf7zgc0D1zRXxLcD6GcM=/pHQ0NJEi8fdlAvA8sI1RPMPJzCI96DCbZ5doK71t_8s=)._

name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: making sure you're up to date
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | | [](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c0d/622b7b5b/1f35a768/307b437f/2777117090/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/RepDebbieWassermanSchultz?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=1&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1&source=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1")
Friend,

I wanted to take a moment to tell you how much I appreciate your support. Because of you, we not only hit our first EOQ fundraising goal of 2019 - we surpassed it.

I wanted to thank you personally. Please click below to watch a video I recorded just for you.

[](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c0d/622b7b5b/1f35a768/307b437e/2777117090/VEsE/ "https://youtu.be/zgrc11cKQHQ?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=2&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1&source=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1")
If you haven't yet, be sure to follow me on [**_Facebook_**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c0d/622b7b5b/1f35a768/307b4371/2777117090/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/RepDebbieWassermanSchultz?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=3+-+Facebook&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1&source=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1") and [**_Twitter_**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c0d/622b7b5b/1f35a768/307b4370/2777117090/VEsC/ "https://twitter.com/DWStweets?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=4+-+Twitter&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1&source=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1") to keep in touch.

Thanks again, for everything. More soon.

Best,

Debbie Wasserman Schultz
---

|
---
| | Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
---
This email was sent to [dpwillis67@gmail.com](mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com).
[Click here to unsubscribe](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113c0d/622b7b5b/1f35a768/307b4373/2777117090/VEsD/ "https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=schultz&utm_content=5+-+Click+here+to+unsubscribe&utm_campaign=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1&source=DWS_EM_TY_90DO_190408_1_1")

name: Chris Evans, Beto for America
subject: 42 cities
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Beto for America
body:
We’ve only been in the race a few short weeks (24 days if you're counting like I am), and Beto has already visited more cities and towns than any other candidate. Even more than those who have been running for months. The New York Times just mapped it all out, calling Beto’s work ethic a “dizzying itinerary”:

#
We’ve only been in the race a few short weeks (24 days if you're counting like I am), and Beto has already visited more cities and towns than any other candidate. Even more than those who have been running for months. _The New York Times_ just mapped it all out, calling Beto’s work ethic a “dizzying itinerary”:
We are going to win this campaign by meeting with, listening to, and learning from people in every community. On the ground work. No one taken for granted and no one left behind. With Beto on the road, and volunteers organizing in every community, we can build the largest grassroots campaign this country has ever seen. We’re absolutely going to need it if we want to defeat Donald Trump and deliver on our vision.
[Can you make a $3 donation to keep Beto out on the road visiting communities and ensure our campaign has the resources we need to win? Every contribution helps us run a competitive grassroots campaign without the backing of PACs, special interests, corporations, or lobbyists.](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17363?refcode=ema20190408-26891&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=19&akid=26891%2E444211%2EDobZTi)
**[Contribute $3 »](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/17363?refcode=ema20190408-26891&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=26891%2E444211%2EDobZTi)**
Wherever you are in the United States, we want you to be part of this. A campaign _by_ all of us _for_ all of us.
Let's show Beto we’ll work for him like he's working for us.
Chris Evans
Beto for America

Paid for by Beto for America
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. This campaign is powered by grassroots supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [you can do so here](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/). If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=21&akid=26891%2E444211%2EDobZTi). To contribute via check, please address to Beto for America campaign, P.O. Box 3628 El Paso, TX 79923.

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: How President Trump’s Border Policies Are Boosting Wage Growth
body:
"The numbers don’t lie: The double whammy of lower taxes and a decline in the importing of low-wage illegal workers is making life better for..." | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4b136231a8&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **How President Trump’s Border Policies Are Boosting Wage Growth**

“The left won’t admit it, but Friday’s employment report is yet more proof that President Trump’s policies have achieved in a little more than one year something progressive politicians have been championing for nearly forever: a fairly significant increase in the federal minimum wage,” Charles Gasparino writes in the _New York Post_. “Trump hasn’t officially agreed to raise the federal minimum wage above its current level of $7.25 an hour, but then again, he doesn’t have to. His policies are doing it all for him — including restricting illegal immigration.”

“The numbers don’t lie: The double whammy of lower taxes and a decline in the importing of low-wage illegal workers is making life better for the blue collar.”

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7f2e727ac4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Even the media finally admit there’s a crisis at our southern border and that Mexico is moving to address it. We should all thank President Trump for prompting Mexico to act with his threat to close the border. But most journalists won’t give Trump credit for anything,” Dan Gainor writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=04ed77956e&e=3ee4985eea). “Instead, many in the media are blaming the president for the border crisis that he is working to solve.”
---
|
---
| “In the closing months of Barack Obama’s presidency — at the height of a national election — high-level government officials with access to terrifying loads of clandestine information about American citizens began spying on domestic political opponents,” Charles Hurt writes in _[The Washington Times](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=bef1766dbf&e=3ee4985eea)_. The ensuing investigation “has done nothing but vindicate Mr. Trump and his valid concern that the Obama administration turned America’s clandestine services against domestic political opponents during an election. This is truly terrifying stuff.”
---
|
---
| “Unlike the 1990s – when 90 percent of the border crossers were turned around and sent from whence they came – the new migrants are families who intentionally walk up to our border agents and get released into the U.S. after simply saying the magic word ‘asylum,’” Steve Levy writes in [Fox News](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d36d67a87f&e=3ee4985eea). “So here’s the question: Why aren’t Democrats being held accountable for the border crisis or for blocking asylum reform?”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d3b68333b7&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ea3d850d6f&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=c30b911bc9)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

His policies are doing it all for him — including restricting illegal immigration.” “The numbers don’t lie: The double whammy of lower taxes and a decline in the importing of low-wage illegal workers is making life better for the blue collar.” | | “Even the media finally admit there’s a crisis at our southern border and that Mexico is moving to address it. We should all thank President Trump for prompting Mexico to act with his threat to close the border. name: Kamala Harris
subject: Decisions
body:
I’m preparing for our
campaign’s planning retreat and wanted to check in with you.

Early next week, our team will convene at our headquarters to plan out the
next phase of our campaign. We will discuss everything from the upcoming
debates to building out our organizing efforts on the ground in
communities across the country.

Before Friday, we need to lock down our latest fundraising numbers so we
can have the most up-to-date information going into this meeting.

So I’m asking, can you add a donation to our campaign? Without
your help, we won’t be able to make the critical investments needed to
keep our momentum strong.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&express_lane=true&t=1001&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $5 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=25&express_lane=true&t=1002&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $25 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=50&express_lane=true&t=1003&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $50 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=100&express_lane=true&t=1004&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $100 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=250&express_lane=true&t=1005&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $250 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1006&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Another Amount

Since we launched our historic campaign in January, we’ve been working
hard to share our positive message. None of what we’ve accomplished so far
would be possible if it weren’t for our supporters pitching in your time
and your donations of whatever amount you can afford. Our team knows that
we can count on you no matter what -- which is why I’m reaching out today.

It’s time to lock in the next steps for our campaign, and we need to set
big goals to keep up the pace. What we decide at this retreat -- about
staffing, organizing tools, budget, and much more -- will determine the
course of the coming months, especially with the first round of debates in
June.

We need to make some important calls on the future of our campaign before
the weekend and it starts with our finances. Pitch in your first contribution to help us
solidify our plans for the fight ahead.

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation will go through immediately:

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=5&express_lane=true&t=1007&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $5 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=25&express_lane=true&t=1008&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $25 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=50&express_lane=true&t=1009&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $50 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=100&express_lane=true&t=1010&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $100 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amount=250&express_lane=true&t=1011&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Chip in $250 now

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kdh-ema-190408-midmonth?refcode=ema190408-full&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&t=1012&refcode2=4235_5755789_gKhFVW&akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW ]Another Amount

Thanks for all you do for our campaign.

For The People,

— Kamala Harris

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://action.kamalaharris.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=4235%2E5755789%2EgKhFVW

name: Erica, Kirsten (3)
subject: RE: Just got back from NH
body:
| Making sure you saw this, Peter--we're making solid progress, but Kirsten's CNN town hall is _tomorrow_ , and we still have to bring in 944 new donors between now and then. This is _the_ moment when we need your help to show that a people-powered campaign can break through and win.

**But we can't do it without people like you bravely stepping forward and making your first donation in time: [Donate $5 or more right now to get Kirsten on the debate stage and keep this community-led campaign surging ahead to defeat Trump. No corporate PACs, no federal lobbyists, no fossil fuel PAC money, no super PAC--Kirsten is counting on _you_.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976e64/2ae112ef/6c32e6ac/357dd460/1543400762/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwMTctZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3017-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
---
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Kirsten
Date: Sun, Apr 07, 2019
Subject: Just got back from NH and want to check in on something
Peter,
I just got back from my latest trip to New Hampshire and want to check in with you on something really quick. I know our team is working overtime to bring in 5,000 new donors before I take the stage for my CNN town hall Tuesday night.
**[I have to ask: Can you become a founding member of our team by making your first donation to my campaign before Tuesday night? Your gift helps keep me out on the road meeting voters and building the strongest people-powered campaign possible.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976e64/2ae112ef/6c32e6ac/357dd460/1543400762/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwMTctZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3017-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
_If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
**[Click to donate $5.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3017-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&express_lane=true&amount=5")**
**[Click to donate $25.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3017-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&express_lane=true&amount=25")**
**[Click to donate $50.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3017-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&express_lane=true&amount=50")**
**[Or click here to donate another amount.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976e64/2ae112ef/6c32e6ac/357dd460/1543400762/VEsA/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwMTctZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3017-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")**
We're not taking money from corporate PACs, federal lobbyists or fossil fuel executives, and we don't have an individual super PAC. So my ability to meet folks on the road and ask them in person to be a part of this campaign is really, really important.
I have the honor and privilege to hear from them about the issues that are on their minds--most often, it's the rising cost of health care, keeping our schools and communities safe from gun violence and protecting our environment for the next generation. And I'm looking forward to addressing these issues and more on Tuesday night.
**Before I take the stage, please give any amount you can --$1, $5 or $10--to become one of 5,000 new donors we're aiming to bring in before Tuesday. Every dollar helps:**
[**https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email**](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976e64/2ae112ef/6c32e6ac/357dd460/1543400762/VEsB/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwMTctZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3017-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")
I hope you can tune in Tuesday night!
Thanks for everything,
Kirsten
P.S. Here's a photo from our meet-and-greet in Dover this weekend. I love meeting voters on the road and hope to meet you soon--if I haven't yet!
[](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976e64/2ae112ef/6c32e6ac/357dd460/1543400762/VEsO/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5Ijoia2ctZW1haWwiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6Imp0azMwMTctZnItZ2ZwLW5kIiwiZTNzZ1RGQkJRVkYxWlhKNVUzUnlhVzVuSUgxOSI6IjUsMjUsNTAsMTAwLDI1MCw1MDAiLCJlM3NnWVdKeVpXTjFjbkpwYm1jZ2ZYMD0iOiIifQ==/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/kg-email?refcode=jtk3017-fr-gfp-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from the Gillibrand 2020 email list, please click here.](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f976e64/2ae112ef/6c32e6ac/357dd46c/1543400762/VEsP/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "https://gillibrand.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

Paid for by Gillibrand 2020
Contributions or gifts to Gillibrand 2020 are not tax-deductible. Gillibrand 2020 does not accept contributions from federal lobbyists, corporate and/or trade association PACs, or "for profit" business entities, including but not limited to sole proprietorships, partnerships, and LLCs.

But we can't do it without people like you bravely stepping forward and making your first donation in time: "" \---------- Forwarded message ---------- From: Kirsten Date: Sun, Apr 07, 2019 Subject: Just got back from NH and want to check in on something Peter, I just got back from my latest trip to New Hampshire and want to check in with you on something really quick. name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump takes action today on Iran
body:
The Iranian regime continues to lead a campaign of terrorism around the world. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6dc6d61bd4&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • April 8, 2019
---
| | |
# **President Trump takes action today on Iran**

**The Iranian regime** continues to lead a campaign of terrorism around the world. Today, President Donald J. Trump took historic action to confront the Iranian threat by designating the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a Foreign Terrorist Organization.

**The** **President** **explained** in an official statement that this unprecedented step “recognizes the reality that Iran is not only a State Sponsor of Terrorism, but that the IRGC actively participates in, finances, and promotes terrorism as a tool of statecraft.”

**That designation** marks the first time America has labeled part of another government a terrorist organization. “It underscores the fact that Iran’s actions are fundamentally different from those of other governments. This action will significantly expand the scope and scale of our maximum pressure on the Iranian regime,” President Trump says.

**For years,** Iran has actively employed terrorism as a central tool in its foreign policy. The head of that snake has been the IRGC. Today, the regime in Tehran remains the world’s leading state sponsor of terror, funneling nearly a billion dollars each year toward radical groups such as Hezbollah, Hamas, and Palestinian Islamic Jihad.

**Since his earliest days** in office, President Trump has worked to counter the regime’s malign activities and ensure that it never gets its hands on a nuclear weapon.

**Withdrawing** from the Obama Administration’s unacceptable Iran deal was a key step toward that end. The Iran agreement represented a categorical failure in American diplomacy, lifting crucial sanctions on Iran and thus providing it with the revenue stream needed to fund even more terrorist activities.

**Now, those sanctions** are back—coupled with even tougher ones. Today’s action ends the era of plausible deniability for Iran and sends a clear message that their support for terrorism has serious consequences. It also lets the rest of the world know where the United States stands in no uncertain terms.

[President Trump’s statement on designating the IRGC a Foreign Terrorist Organization](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0782661e0c&e=3ee4985eea)

**Get the facts:** [President Trump is holding the Iranian regime accountable ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4ef61b28c5&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Video of the day: President Trump visits the border wall**
---
| [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4113e26083&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| **On Friday,** President Trump traveled to Calexico, California, to see the first completed section of the new wall along the U.S.–Mexico border. The newly-constructed 30-foot bollard wall replaces a barrier made of surplus corrugated steel landing mats from the Vietnam War.

**The visit** also allowed the President to hear directly from individuals who work to keep America safe every day. They understand this growing border crisis better than any politician. And one thing that President Trump hears from border officials on the ground time and time again: The new barrier is exactly what they need.

**“We’re very appreciative** of this wall,” Gloria Chavez, Chief Patrol Agent of the El Centro Sector, told President Trump. “These men and women, out here in this area of two miles, were experiencing a high number of assaults and use-of-force incidents.”

**Since the new border wall** has gone up, those assault incidents have plunged by 65 percent, she added. “In these two miles, just illegal entries alone have dropped by 75 percent.”
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead
| **U.S. Border Patrol officials listen to President Trump speak on Friday during his visit to Calexico, California** | April 5, 2019
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=877c9ef928&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=46d278b11b&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=fff2d6dfb7)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---
